Documentation.Summary: Called once per component

ConstructorArguments: Called once, when the component or gameobject is destroyed

Documentation.Summary: Called once, when the component or gameobject is destroyed

ConstructorArguments: When enabled, called every frame, does not get called on a dedicated server

Documentation.Summary: When enabled, called every frame, does not get called on a dedicated server

ConstructorArguments: Destroy this component, if it isn't already destroyed. The component will be removed from its GameObject and will stop existing. You should avoid interating with the component after calling this.

Documentation.Summary:
Destroy this component, if it isn't already destroyed. The component will be removed from its
GameObject and will stop existing. You should avoid interating with the component after calling this.

ConstructorArguments: Destroy the parent GameObject. This really only exists so when you're typing Destroy you realise that calling Destroy only destroys the Component - not the whole GameObject.

Documentation.Summary:
Destroy the parent GameObject. This really only exists so when you're typing Destroy you realise
that calling Destroy only destroys the Component - not the whole GameObject.

ConstructorArguments: Called immediately after deserializing, and when a property is changed in the editor.

ConstructorArguments: Called immediately after being refreshed from a network snapshot.

ConstructorArguments: Deserialize this component as per Component.Deserialize(JsonObject) but update Component.GameObject and Component property references immediately instead of having them deferred.

Documentation.Summary:
Deserialize this component as per <see cref="M:Sandbox.Component.Deserialize(System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonObject)" /> but update <see cref="P:Sandbox.Component.GameObject" /> and <see cref="T:Sandbox.Component" /> property
references immediately instead of having them deferred.

ConstructorArguments: Called once before the first Update - when enabled.

Documentation.Summary: Called once before the first Update - when enabled.
When enabled, called every frame

ConstructorArguments: When enabled, called on a fixed interval that is determined by the Scene. This is also the fixed interval in which the physics are ticked. Time.Delta is that fixed interval.

Documentation.Summary:
When enabled, called on a fixed interval that is determined by the Scene. This
is also the fixed interval in which the physics are ticked. Time.Delta is that
fixed interval.

ConstructorArguments: The scene this Component is in. This is a shortcut for `GameObject.Scene`.

Documentation.Summary: The scene this Component is in. This is a shortcut for `GameObject.Scene`.

ConstructorArguments: The transform of the GameObject this component belongs to. Components don't have their own transforms but they can access the transform of the GameObject they belong to. This is a shortcut for `GameObject.Transform`.

Documentation.Summary:
The transform of the GameObject this component belongs to. Components don't have their own transforms

ConstructorArguments:
The enable state of this Component.

This doesn't tell you whether the component is actually active because its parent GameObject might be disabled. This merely tells you what the component wants to be. You should use Component.Active to determine whether the object is truly active in the scene.

Documentation.Summary:
<para>
            The enable state of this <see cref="T:Sandbox.Component" />.
            </para><para>
            This doesn't tell you whether the component is actually active because its parent
            <see cref="T:Sandbox.GameObject" /> might be disabled. This merely tells you what the
            component wants to be. You should use <see cref="P:Sandbox.Component.Active" /> to determine whether the
            object is truly active in the scene.
            </para>
